questions.question_text: Atmospheric air at 1 atm, dry bulb temperature of 15C and wet bulb temperature of 11C enters a heating coil whose temperature is 41C. If the by-pass ratio is 0.4, determine the dry bulb temperature and relative humidity at the exit. Also determine the sensible heat added to the air per kg of dry air
solution: Solution (expanded, step-by-step following the method shown in the image):
1) Write down the given data (as in the notes). These are the conditions of the inlet air and the coil:
- By-pass ratio (fraction bypassing the coil): $x=0.4$ (i.e. 40% bypasses the coil, 60% goes through the coil)
- Use psychrometric chart / property table to obtain state 1 (inlet) and state 2 (air after passing the coil) properties. From the notes (values read from the chart):
- Enthalpy of air leaving the coil (state 2): $h_2=58\ \mathrm{kJ\,/\,kg_{dry\,air}}$
- Specific humidity after coil: $\omega_2=\omega_1=6.4\ \mathrm{g\,/\,kg}$ (heating is sensible only, no moisture change)
Note: the values $\omega_1$, $h_1$, $h_2$ are taken from the psychrometric chart or table as done in the handwritten solution.
2) Set up the mass (moisture) balance and enthalpy (energy) balance for the mixing (by-pass) of the two air streams to form the exit air (state 3).
- Mass (humidity) balance for water vapour (per kg dry air basis):
- Enthalpy (mixing) balance (per kg dry air): the mixed-stream enthalpy $h_3$ is a mass-weighted average of the bypass (state 1) and the heated (state 2) enthalpies:
3) Compute the mixed-stream enthalpy $h_3$ (substitute given numeric values).
4) Determine the exit dry-bulb temperature $T_{d3}$ and relative humidity (RH) from the psychrometric chart using the known pair $(h_3,\,\omega_3)$.
- We now have for state 3: enthalpy $h_3=47.4\ \mathrm{kJ/kg}$ and specific humidity $\omega_3=6.4\ \mathrm{g/kg}$.

- Using the psychrometric chart (or psychrometric relations), locate the point with $\omega=6.4\ \mathrm{g/kg}$ and $h=47.4\ \mathrm{kJ/kg}$ (or follow the mixing line on the chart). From the chart-reading done in the notes, this corresponds approximately to:
and the relative humidity at the exit is approximately
- (Explanation: $h_3$ determines the position along enthalpy lines; with $\omega_3$ fixed, read the dry-bulb temperature and relative humidity at that intersection on the psychrometric chart.)

5) Compute the sensible heat added to the air per kg of dry air. Explain the energy accounting: only the fraction $(1-x)$ of the dry-air flow actually passes through the heating coil and receives the enthalpy rise $h_2-h_1$. Therefore the sensible energy added per kg dry air of the overall supply (mixed) equals $(1-x)(h_2-h_1)$. (This is the same formula used in the notes.)
Summary of results (as obtained in the handwritten solution):
- Sensible heat added (per kg dry air): $q_{sensible}\approx 15.9\ \mathrm{kJ/kg}$.

- The solution follows the same procedure and numeric chart values used in the image: read $h_1$, $\omega_1$ from the psychrometric chart for the inlet conditions, read $h_2$ for the air after the coil, apply mixing (mass and enthalpy) to get $h_3$, then read $T_{d3}$ and RH from the chart at the point $(h_3,\omega_3)$.
- All enthalpy values are per kg of dry air ($\mathrm{kJ/kg_{dry\,air}}$) and specific humidity in g/kg (as used in the chart/notes).

- The algebraic steps and numerical substitutions are shown explicitly above to make the derivation generalizable to other numeric inputs (replace the numeric property values read from the psychrometric chart to solve other cases).
